description: Submodule Support & Scoped Queries - Git submodules scanned into separate DBs with scope-based query routing in all MCP tools

userStories.title: Migration V5: submodules + repo_metadata tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables to store submodule relationships and cached metadata so that submodule DBs can be linked to their parent project.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration V5__add_submodules.sql created with submodules table (id, relative_path UNIQUE, name, db_path, commit_hash, created_at, updated_at) and repo_metadata table (key PRIMARY KEY, value)
Migration applied successfully to in-memory DB in tests
seshat-storage: SubmoduleRepository trait with insert/update/delete/list/find_by_path methods + SqliteSubmoduleRepository implementation
seshat-storage: RepoMetadataRepository trait with get/set/get_all methods + SqliteRepoMetadataRepository implementation
Unit tests: CRUD on submodules table, write/read repo_metadata key-value pairs
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Pure storage layer. No scanning logic yet. Tables are created in ALL Seshat DBs (root and submodule) since migrations run on Database::open().

userStories.title: Submodule DB path resolution + ScanConfig field rename
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities for resolving submodule DB paths and an inverted config default so that submodules are scanned by default.
acceptanceCriteria: db.rs: new resolve_submodule_db_path(project_name, mount_path) -> PathBuf returns repos/{project_name}/{mount_path}.db
db.rs: parent directories created automatically via std::fs::create_dir_all
ScanConfig in seshat-core: rename include_submodules -> exclude_submodules (bool, default false, #[serde(default)])
args.rs: replace --include-submodules with --exclude-submodules on Command::Scan
scan.rs: update config override logic for the new field name
discovery.rs: invert filter logic (exclude_submodules = true means skip submodules from root walk)
seshat.example.toml: update comment and field name
detect_submodule_paths() in discovery.rs made pub(crate) or pub
All existing tests updated for renamed field
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Breaking change in config field name. Existing seshat.toml with include_submodules will get serde default (false = scan submodules). Root discovery ALWAYS excludes submodule dirs (they get their own DBs); exclude_submodules controls whether separate submodule scans happen.

userStories.title: ScanProgress submodule variants + get_submodule_commit_hash()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need progress events for submodule phases and a function to get the current commit hash of a submodule.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 new ScanProgress variants added: SubmoduleDetected, ScanningSubmodule, SubmoduleScanDone, SubmoduleUpToDate, SubmoduleSkipped
New function get_submodule_commit_hash(submodule_path: &Path) -> Option<String> in seshat-scanner (runs git rev-parse HEAD or reads .git file)
scan.rs progress callback handles new variants with spinners (SubmoduleDetected shows info line, ScanningSubmodule shows spinner per submodule, SubmoduleUpToDate shows checkmark with hash)
Existing ScanProgress match arms unchanged (wildcard _ for new variants in tests that don't care)
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: This prepares the infrastructure for the actual submodule scan flow. No scanning logic yet — just the progress events and git hash utility.

userStories.title: Submodule scan flow in scan.rs (N+1 orchestrator calls)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want seshat scan to detect submodules and scan each into its own DB so that conventions are isolated per submodule.
acceptanceCriteria: scan.rs: after root discovery, parse .gitmodules via detect_submodule_paths()
For each submodule: check if initialized (non-empty dir with .git), skip with SubmoduleSkipped if not
For each initialized submodule: open/create DB via resolve_submodule_db_path(), call scan_project_with_progress() with submodule path and submodule DB
After each submodule scan: write repo_metadata (parent_project, mount_path, file_count, convention_count, last_scan_time) to submodule DB
Root project scanned after submodules (excluding submodule dirs from discovery — already implemented)
Update submodules table in root DB with mount_path, name, db_path, commit_hash for each scanned submodule
Write repo_metadata (project_name, file_count, convention_count, last_scan_time) to root DB
Scan output shows submodule progress via new ScanProgress variants
Integration test: scan fixture project with mock submodule dir → verify two .db files created with correct structure
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Core engineering story. Reuses scan_project_with_progress() as-is for each submodule — just different root path and DB. Parallel scanning deferred to separate story for simplicity.

userStories.title: Submodule change detection (commit_hash compare)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want seshat scan to skip unchanged submodules based on commit hash so that rescans are fast.
acceptanceCriteria: On scan: for each submodule, compare get_submodule_commit_hash() with stored commit_hash from submodules table
If hash matches: emit SubmoduleUpToDate, skip scan
If hash differs: full rescan of submodule DB, update commit_hash in submodules table
If submodule is new (not in table): full scan, insert into submodules table
If submodule removed from .gitmodules: delete row from submodules table, leave orphaned DB on disk
Progress output shows up-to-date vs rescanning with hash info
Unit tests: mock submodules table with known hashes, verify skip/rescan logic
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Builds on US-004. Adds the optimization layer — only rescan when commit changes.

userStories.title: Parallel submodule scanning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want submodules scanned in parallel so that projects with multiple submodules don't take N times longer.
acceptanceCriteria: Submodule scans run in parallel via rayon::scope or std::thread::scope
Each submodule gets its own spinner line in scan output (concurrent progress updates)
Root scan still runs after all submodules complete (needs submodule exclusion list)
Thread safety: each submodule has its own DB connection, no shared mutable state
Performance: 2 submodules should take ~1.2x single submodule time, not 2x
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Optimization story. Can be skipped initially if sequential scanning is acceptable. rayon::scope preferred since scan pipeline already uses rayon internally.

userStories.title: scope.rs module + ErrorCode variants
userStories.description: As a system architect, I need a scope resolution module and new error codes so that MCP tools can route queries to the correct knowledge graph.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-mcp/src/scope.rs with resolve_scope() function
resolve_scope(scope, file_path, root, submodules, mount_paths) -> Result<(&ProjectConnection, String), ErrorCode>
Priority: explicit scope > file_path prefix match (longest wins) > default root
Explicit scope: full mount path direct lookup, short name fallback with ambiguity check
scope 'root' always returns root connection
New ProjectConnection struct: conn + name + branch
ErrorCode enum: add UnknownScope and RepoNotFound variants
Unit tests: 8+ cases covering explicit scope, file_path auto-detect, default root, ambiguous short name, unknown scope, longest prefix match
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Pure logic module, no McpServer changes yet. ProjectConnection and resolve_scope are standalone testable units.

userStories.title: McpServer redesign: root + submodules HashMap
userStories.description: As a developer, I need McpServer to hold root + submodule connections and delegate to resolve_scope so that all tool handlers can serve scoped queries.
acceptanceCriteria: McpServer struct: replace single conn/repo_name/branch with root: ProjectConnection + submodules: HashMap<String, ProjectConnection> + mount_paths: Vec<String>
McpServer::new() takes root ProjectConnection + HashMap of submodule connections
start_stdio() and start_stdio_with_shutdown() signatures updated to accept new parameters
All 5 tool handlers updated: call self.resolve_scope(scope, file_path) to get (conn, scope_name), use conn for graph calls, scope_name for envelope
Backward compatible: empty submodules HashMap = single-project mode, all queries go to root
Existing MCP tests updated for new constructor signature
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: This is the biggest refactoring story in the MCP crate. Touches server.rs + all 5 tool handlers. But each handler change is mechanical: add resolve_scope call, use returned conn.

userStories.title: file_path parameter in all 5 MCP tool schemas
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to pass file_path to any MCP tool for automatic scope routing so that I get correct results without manually specifying scope.
acceptanceCriteria: file_path: Option<String> added to all 5 request structs (ProjectContextRequest, QueryConventionRequest, RecordDecisionRequest, UpdateDecisionRequest, RemoveDecisionRequest)
schemars description: 'File path relative to project root. Used for automatic scope detection — if the file belongs to a submodule, the query/write targets that submodule's knowledge graph.'
file_path normalization: strip leading '/' or './', treat as relative to project root
All handlers pass file_path to resolve_scope()
Write tools: record_decision with file_path in submodule dir → decision written to submodule DB
Tool descriptions in #[tool(description)] updated to mention file_path
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Builds on US-008 (McpServer redesign). Mechanical addition to all request structs + handler wiring.

userStories.title: seshat serve with submodule connections
userStories.description: As a developer, I want seshat serve to load root + submodule connections at startup so that AI agents can query any scope immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: serve.rs: after loading root DB, read submodules table
For each submodule: resolve DB path, open connection, build ProjectConnection
Build HashMap<String, ProjectConnection> keyed by mount_path
Pass root + submodules to McpServer::new()
Startup output shows submodule count and names
If submodule DB missing on disk: log warning, skip that submodule (don't crash)
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Wires everything together. After this story, the full scan → serve → query cycle works with submodules.

userStories.title: seshat status command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want seshat status to show a tree of all projects and submodules so that I can manage my Seshat databases.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/seshat-cli/src/status.rs implementing run_status(verbose: bool)
Scan XDG repos dir for .db files, identify root projects vs submodules (root = has no parent dir, submodule = inside a project dir)
For each DB: read repo_metadata table for summary (file_count, convention_count, last_scan_time)
Tree output with aligned columns: name, branch, files, conventions, DB size, last modified (human-readable)
Orphaned submodule DBs shown with warning indicator
Full DB paths with --verbose flag
args.rs: add --verbose to Command::Status
lib.rs: replace status stub with call to status::run_status()
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Independent from MCP tools. Can read repo_metadata without opening full DB if we store summary in the KV table.

userStories.title: repo parameter activation
userStories.description: As an AI agent developer, I want the repo parameter validated so that mismatches produce clear errors.
acceptanceCriteria: In all 5 tool handlers: if req.repo is Some and doesn't match self.root.name (case-insensitive), return REPO_NOT_FOUND error
Error message includes loaded project name and hint
Response envelope repo field reflects actual project name
Unit test: call with wrong repo name → REPO_NOT_FOUND
Cargo test passes
Cargo clippy passes
userStories.notes: Lowest priority. Trivial if-statement in each handler. Real value comes with daemon mode (future epic).
